summary:
The patient’s history of chronic smoking and bilateral duct dilation—specifically involving both the main pancreatic duct and common bile duct—are highly suggestive of an upstream mass such as pancreatic head carcinoma. Given the strong association between cigarette smoke exposure and pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma, particularly in older adults, this makes pancreatic head carcinoma the most probable diagnosis despite shared radiographic findings with periampullary malignancies.  

\boxed{\text{A}}

\boxed{\text{A}}. Answer choice: A
